. *--------------------------------------------------
. * Run processing and analysis scripts

. do "$working_ANALYSIS/scripts/01_clean_Bangladesh2018.do"

. *--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
. * SCRIPT: 01_clean_Bangladesh2018.do
. * PURPOSE: cleans the raw excel data from the 2018 data collection and 
. * generates additional variables for analysis
. *--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
. 
. *----------------------------
. * 1) Import excel dataset and cleaning
. *----------------------------
. *--------------------------------------------------
. * Description
. *--------------------------------------------------
. /*
> This do-files cleans household surveys conducted in Bangladesh in 2018.
> 
>  1) Initial changes (dropping empty variables and renaming some variables)
>  2) Labeling
>  3) Setup variables (dates, location where data was gathered, ... )
>  4) Socio-economic variables (age, gender, income, assets, ...)
>  5) Priming (priming check variables)
>  6) Confirmation bias
>  7) Climate change (perception of CC, experiences, ...)
>  8) Migration (migration preferences, plans, and experiences)
>  9) Preferences (time & risk preferences, place attachment, ...)
>  10) Network (number of friends and how they are connected with each other)
> 
> */
. *--------------------------------------------------
. 
. 
. clear all

. insheet using "$working_ANALYSIS\data\bangladesh2018.csv", delimiter(";") clear
(477 vars, 247 obs)

. 
. *--------------------------------------------------
. * 1) Initial changes
. {
. *dropping variables that were necessary for programing only
. drop location_note treatment_emotions_note dg_note migration_temp_note migration_temp_note_001 migration_reasons_note migration_reasons_ranking_note mp_note
> 1 mp_note2 migration_abroad_note mc_note mr_note migration_success_note attitudes_note contact_note cc_resettle_note cc_conspiracy_note erosion_note_a erosi
> on_explaination_note_a erosion_animation_note_a accretion_note_a accretion_explaination_note_a accretion_video_note_a accretion_note_b accretion_explainatio
> n_note_b accretion_video_note_b erosion_note_b erosion_explaination_note_b erosion_animation_note_b friends_names_note friends_characteristics_note intro_li
> vestock_note intro_immovables_note intro_movables_note thanks_note payout_dg_note payout_spite_note1 payout_spite_note2
. drop intro_living intro_living_prev intro_home treatment_emotions_intro dg_intro_0 dg_intro spite_intro risk_intro_0 risk_intro t_intro intro_willingness in
> tro_faith_government intro_ccperception intro_expectation intro_trust_information intro_cc_contribution info_placeattachment  _uuid _id assistant_notes

. *--------------------------------------------------
. * 3) Setup
. {
. gen country_id=2  //data set identifier
. 
. *Lenght of survey
. split start, p("T" "." "+") gen(start_)
variables created as string: 
start_1  start_2  start_3  start_4
. drop start_3 start_4
. gen double clock1 = clock(start_2,"hms")
. gen start_time = clock1 /1000 /60
. 
. split end, p("T" "." "+") gen(end_)
variables created as string: 
end_1  end_2  end_3  end_4
. drop end_3 end_4
. gen double clock2 = clock(end_2,"hms")
. gen end_time = clock2 /1000 /60
. 
. gen duration = end_time - start_time
. label var duration "Lenght of survey in minutes"
. drop start end clock1 clock2 start_time end_time
. 
. rename start_1 start_date
. rename start_2 start_time
. rename end_1 end_date
. rename end_2 end_time
. 
. *Locations: villages that are affected by coastal erosions and not
. *surveys with affected people on dates: 30.08, 31.08 & 01.09
. *surveys with less affected people: 27.08, 28.08, 29.08, 02.09 & 03.09
. gen location=0 if date=="2018-08-27" |date=="2018-08-28" | date=="2018-08-29"  | date=="2018-09-02" | date=="2018-09-03" 
(119 missing values generated)
. replace location=1 if date=="2018-08-30" |date=="2018-08-31" | date=="2018-09-01"
(119 real changes made)
. lab def loc1 0 "less affected" 1 "strongly affected"
. lab val location loc1
. rename location affected
. 
. *Assisstants
. tab assistant, gen(assi_)

    Name of |
  assistant |
        who |
  contucted |
        the |
  interview |      Freq.     Percent        Cum.
------------+-----------------------------------
        Abu |         30       12.15       12.15
     Sabbir |         32       12.96       25.10
      Sonia |         31       12.55       37.65
Assistant 4 |         31       12.55       50.20
Assistant 5 |         30       12.15       62.35
Assistant 6 |         30       12.15       74.49
Assistant 7 |         31       12.55       87.04
Assistant 8 |         32       12.96      100.00
------------+-----------------------------------
      Total |        247      100.00
. 
. *Payout
. rename payout payout_selection
.         label var payout_selection "Decision to pay out"
.         label define payout_selection 1 "Dictator Game" 2 "Spite" 3 "Risk"
.         label values payout_selection payout_selection
. gen payout_spite = 120
.         replace payout_spite = 110 if spite == 1
(4 real changes made)
.         label var payout_spite "Payout Spite"
. gen payout_risk = 128
.         replace payout_risk = 240 if r1==0 & risk_win==1
(39 real changes made)
.         replace payout_risk = 0 if r1==0 & risk_win==0
(24 real changes made)
.         label var payout_risk "Payout Risk"
. gen payout = 0
.         replace payout = payout_dg if payout_selection==1
(68 real changes made)
.         replace payout = payout_spite if payout_selection==2
(92 real changes made)
.         replace payout = payout_risk if payout_selection==3
(68 real changes made)
.         label var payout "Payout from this survey"
. gen payout_next = 0
.         replace payout_next = -30 if payout_selection==2 & spite==1
(2 real changes made)
.         replace payout_next = dg if payout_selection==1
(56 real changes made)
.         label var payout_next "Payout effects on next survey"
. gen total_payout = payout + payout_next
. 
. 
. 
. *union
. gen new_1=.
(247 missing values generated)
. tostring new_1, generate(new_union)
new_union generated as str1
. replace new_union="Padri Shibpur" if (date=="2018-08-27"|date=="2018-08-28")
variable new_union was str1 now str13
(15 real changes made)

variable new_union was str13 now str17
(40 real changes made)

(39 real changes made)
. drop new_1
. order new_union, after(union)
. label variable new_union "Union where the survey took place"
. rename new_union location
. 
.

. gen ma_afford=0
. replace ma_afford=1 if asset_sum > mc_abroad
(173 real changes made)
. replace ma_afford=. if migrate_abroad==0

. *living preferences

. *Migration likelihood abroad next 3 years
. replace ma_likeliness=. if migrate_abroad==0
(30 real changes made, 30 to missing)
. lab def likely 1 "1='very unlikely'"  7 "7='very likely'", replace
. lab val ma_likeliness likely
. 
. *Emigration costs from Bangladesh Remittances Survey (2009): MENA = 199844 BDT; East Asian country = 228100 BDT; but South Kora / Singapore = 325000 BDT;  N
> orth America = 277834 BDT; Europe = 510000 (australia similar), SOuth Asia (india, pakistan)= nearly no costs (25000 for Pakistan)
. gen actual_costs=278000 if ma_region==1
(209 missing values generated)
. replace actual_costs=510000 if ma_region==2
(27 real changes made)
. replace actual_costs=25000 if ma_region==3
(25 real changes made)
. replace actual_costs=320000 if ma_region==4
(26 real changes made)
. replace actual_costs=510000 if ma_region==5
(3 real changes made)
. replace actual_costs=200000 if ma_region==6
(97 real changes made)
. replace actual_costs=300000 if ma_region==7
(1 real change made)
. 
. *adjust for inflation and PPP: 
. * PPP conversion factor from World Bank, 2018: 30.73048114
. 
. *average inflation Bangladesh: 2009 - 2018 = 9 years = 9*5.6%=50% roughly cumulative inflation rate
. gen ia_migration_costs = (actual_costs*1.5)/30.73048114
(30 missing values generated)
. 
. 
. *Perceived individual affordability, householdsize adjusted
. gen ma_afford_hhsize_adj=0 if mc_abroad > asset_sum_adjusted_hh
(123 missing values generated)
. replace ma_afford_hhsize_adj=1 if mc_abroad < asset_sum_adjusted_hh
(123 real changes made)
. replace ma_afford_hhsize_adj=0 if migrate_abroad==1 & mc_abroad==.
(0 real changes made)
. replace ma_afford_hhsize_adj=. if migrate_abroad==0
(30 real changes made, 30 to missing)
. lab def afford_lab 0 "Can't afford" 1 "Can afford", replace
. lab val ma_afford_hhsize_adj afford_lab
. 
. *Average affordability by region
. bysort ma_region: egen avg_mc_abroad = mean(mc_abroad)
. gen avg_ma_afford = 0 if asset_sum_adjusted_hh < avg_mc_abroad
(80 missing values generated)
. replace avg_ma_afford = 1 if asset_sum_adjusted_hh > avg_mc_abroad
(80 real changes made)
. replace avg_ma_afford=0 if migrate_abroad==1 & avg_mc_abroad==.
(0 real changes made)
. replace avg_ma_afford=. if migrate_abroad==0
(30 real changes made, 30 to missing)
. lab val avg_ma_afford afford_lab
. 
. *estimated affordability based on actual labour migration costs
. gen ma_afford_realistic = 0 if ia_migration_costs > asset_sum_adjusted_hh
(48 missing values generated)
. replace ma_afford_realistic = 1 if ia_migration_costs < asset_sum_adjusted_hh
(48 real changes made)
. replace ma_afford_realistic = 0 if migrate_abroad==1 & ia_migration_costs==.
(0 real changes made)
. replace ma_afford_realistic=. if migrate_abroad==0 | ia_migration_costs==.
(30 real changes made, 30 to missing)
. lab val ma_afford_realistic afford_lab
. tab  ma_afford_realistic

. 
. * Affordability
. bysort costliness_destinations: sum mc_abroad

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-> costliness_destinations = No aspiration

-------------+---------------------------------------------------------
   mc_abroad |         30    122.3454    246.9399          0   976.2294

-------------+---------------------------------------------------------
   mc_abroad |         94    15643.73    16860.16          0   48811.47

. 
. gen afford_destination=1 if asset_sum_adjusted_hh < 767 
(218 missing values generated)
. replace afford_destination=2 if asset_sum_adjusted_hh >= 767 & asset_sum_adjusted_hh < 9069
(169 real changes made)
. replace afford_destination=3 if asset_sum_adjusted_hh >= 9069 & asset_sum_adjusted_hh < 20115
(29 real changes made)
. replace afford_destination=4 if asset_sum_adjusted_hh >= 20115 
(20 real changes made)
. replace afford_destination=. if country_id==1 | asset_sum_adjusted_hh==.
(0 real changes made)
. lab def afford3 1 "Nothing" 2 "Low cost (South Asia)" 3 "Medium cost (MENA)" 4 "High cost (NA, EU, AU, EA)", replace
. lab val afford_destination afford3

. *Place
. global p_identity c2a c2b c2c c2d c2e c2f
. global p_depend c2g c2h c2i c2j c2k c2l
. alpha $p_identity

Test scale = mean(unstandardized items)

Average interitem covariance:     .2036777
Number of items in the scale:            6
Scale reliability coefficient:      0.7299
. alpha $p_depend

Test scale = mean(unstandardized items)

Average interitem covariance:     .2922627
Number of items in the scale:            6
Scale reliability coefficient:      0.6195
. gen c2l_reverse = 6-c2l
. label var c2l_reverse "reversal of c2l"
. gen place_identity= (c2a+c2b+c2c+c2d+c2e+c2f)
. label var place_identity "Higher values imply stronger identity with place"
. gen place_dependence= (c2g+c2h+c2i+c2j+c2k)
. label var place_dependence "Higher values imply stronger dependency on place"
. 
. gen place_attachment= (place_identity+place_dependence)/11
. label var place_attachment "Higher values imply stronger attachment to place"
. 
. alpha c2a c2b c2c c2d c2e c2f c2g c2h c2i c2j c2k //0.8066

Test scale = mean(unstandardized items)

Average interitem covariance:     .2526214
Number of items in the scale:           11
Scale reliability coefficient:      0.8066
. pca c2a c2b c2c c2d c2e c2f c2g c2h c2i c2j c2k, mineigen(1) blanks(0.15)  //c2l was excluded. reverse items don't work well.

                                                 Number of comp.  =          2
                                                 Trace            =         11
    Rotation: (unrotated = principal)            Rho              =     0.5221

. predict place_attachment_pca place_dependency_pca, score

    sum of squares(column-loading) = 1

             c2a |   0.3036   -0.2663

. lab var place_attachment_pca "Higher values imply stronger attachment to place"
. lab var place_dependency_pca "Higher values imply stronger place dependency instead of place identity"

. egen z_place=std(place_attachment)
. egen z_identity = std(place_identity)
. egen z_dependence = std(place_dependence)

. *Reciprocity
. egen z_reciprocity = std(reciprocity)

. *LOT-R by Carcer(2013)
. gen lot_score=(c1a+c1c+c1d+c1g+c1i+c1j)

. }

. *--------------------------------------------------
. * 10) Network
. {
. *** Network Data
. /*
> The questions on the network of participants have been programmed to facilitate 
> the interview for interviewers. For all areas of the networks - i.e. close friends,
> friends which are strangers to each other, and friends that are close to each 
> other - variables have been created for the case that a participants names 1, 2,
> 3, 4, or 5 friends. These variables are in the following put together so that for
> all different number of friends the same variable applies.
> Whenever a statement applies - e.g. friend 3 and 4 being strangers to each 
> other - the dummy takes the value 1, if the friends are not strangers it takes
> the value 0, if the participant named 3 friends or less the variable has a missing
> value (.)
> */
. 
. ** Friends especially close
. * Generate variables capturing close friends
. gen friend1_close=.
(247 missing values generated)
. label variable friend1_close "friend 1 especially close"
. gen friend2_close=.
(247 missing values generated)
. label variable friend2_close "friend 2 especially close"
. gen friend3_close=.
(247 missing values generated)
. label variable friend3_close "friend 3 especially close"
. gen friend4_close=.
(247 missing values generated)
. label variable friend4_close "friend 4 especially close"
. gen friend5_close=.
(247 missing values generated)
. label variable friend5_close "friend 5 especially close"
. 
. * Manipulate variable according to information
. replace friend1_close=1 if friends_close51==1 | friends_close41==1 | friends_close31==1 | friends_close21==1
(28 real changes made)
. replace friend1_close=0 if friends_close51==0 | friends_close41==0 | friends_close31==0 | friends_close21==0
(21 real changes made)
. replace friend2_close=1 if friends_close52==1 | friends_close42==1 | friends_close32==1 | friends_close22==1
(14 real changes made)
. replace friend2_close=0 if friends_close52==0 | friends_close42==0 | friends_close32==0 | friends_close22==0
(35 real changes made)
. replace friend3_close=1 if friends_close53==1 | friends_close43==1 | friends_close33==1 
(3 real changes made)
. replace friend3_close=0 if friends_close53==0 | friends_close43==0 | friends_close33==0 
(46 real changes made)
. replace friend4_close=1 if friends_close54==1 | friends_close44==1
(4 real changes made)
. replace friend4_close=0 if friends_close54==0 | friends_close44==0
(38 real changes made)
. replace friend5_close=1 if friends_close55==1
(7 real changes made)
. replace friend5_close=0 if friends_close55==0
(31 real changes made)
. 
. * Drop variables for number-of-friend individual cases
. drop friends_close51 friends_close52 friends_close53 friends_close54 friends_close55 friends_close4 friends_close41 friends_close42 friends_close43 friends_
> close44 friends_close3 friends_close31 friends_close32 friends_close33 friends_close2 friends_close21 friends_close22 friends_close5
. 
. 
. 
. ** Friends being strangers to each other
. * Generate variables capturing friends being strangers to each other

(247 missing values generated)

. * Manipulate variables according to information

(78 real changes made)

. *Lenght of survey
. split start, p("T" "." "+") gen(start_)
variables created as string: 
start_1  start_2  start_3  start_4

. drop start_3 start_4

. gen double clock1 = clock(start_2,"hms")

. gen start_time = clock1 /1000 /60

. 
. split end, p("T" "." "+") gen(end_)
variables created as string: 
end_1  end_2  end_3  end_4

. drop end_3 end_4

. gen double clock2 = clock(end_2,"hms")

. gen end_time = clock2 /1000 /60

. 
. 
. drop start end clock1 clock2 start_time end_time end_1 

. 
. rename start_1 start_date

. lab var start_date "Start date"

. rename start_2 start_time

. lab var start_time "Start time"

. rename end_2 end_time

. lab var end_time "End time"

. 
. gen start_time_stata = clock(start_time, "hms")

. gen end_time_stata=clock(end_time, "hms")

. gen time_sum= (end_time_stata-start_time_stata) / (60000) // milliseconds to minutes: /(60*1000)

. label var time_sum "Lenght of survey in minutes"

.

. * Z-score within each imputation (flong: _mi_m = 0..20)
. foreach var of varlist staircase_risk staircase_patience survey_risk survey_patience {
  2.         bysort _mi_m: egen double mean_`var' = mean(`var')
  3.         bysort _mi_m: egen double sd_`var' = sd(`var')
  4.         gen double z_`var' = (`var' - mean_`var') / sd_`var'
  5.         drop mean_`var' sd_`var'
  6. }
(385 missing values generated)
(385 missing values generated)
(247 missing values generated)
(247 missing values generated)

. 
. * Build composites within each imputation (GPS weights)
. *Patience = 0.7115185 × Staircase patience + 0.2884815 × Will. to give up sth. today
. gen double patience = 0.7115185*z_staircase_patience + 0.2884815*z_survey_patience
(632 missing values generated)

. *Risk taking = 0.4729985 × Staircase risk + 0.5270015 × Will. to take risks
. gen double risktaking = 0.4729985*z_staircase_risk + 0.5270015*z_survey_risk
(632 missing values generated)

. 
. * Min-max normalize within each imputation
. foreach var of varlist patience risktaking {
  2.         bysort _mi_m: egen double min_`var' = min(`var')
  3.         bysort _mi_m: egen double max_`var' = max(`var')
  4.         gen double `var'_norm = (`var' - min_`var') / (max_`var' - min_`var') * 100
  5.         drop min_`var' max_`var'
  6. }
(632 missing values generated)
(632 missing values generated)
(632 missing values generated)
(632 missing values generated)
(632 missing values generated)
(632 missing values generated)

  4.         replace `x'_median = 0 if `x' < aux_`x'
  5.         drop aux_`x'
  6. }

. 
. *Panel B: Motivated reasoning across individuals
. /*
> When analysing and classifying mobility types one needs to be aware that migrants are on average better educated (Drabo and Mbaye, 2015), wealthier (Richard
>  Black et al., 2011a; Bryan et al., 2014; Cattaneo and Peri, 2016), not female (Gray and Mueller, 2012; Mueller et al., 2014), more risk-loving (Jaeger et a
> l., 2010), less patient (Goldbach and Schlüter, 2018), more tolerant of uncertainty (Williams and Baláž, 2012) and have stronger social ties to the destinat
> ion (Beine and Parsons, 2015; Haug, 2008; Manchin and Orazbayev, 2018), and less attachment to the place of origin (Bonaiuto et al., 2016).
> Variables: education, SES, gender, risk-loving, impatient, less place attached
> */
. 
. gen ignore_slr100 = ignore_slr*100

. gen ignore_any= 0 if bias_measurement == 5

. replace ignore_any = 1 if bias_measurement != 5
(102 real changes made)

. gen ignore_any100 = ignore_any*100

. *median splits of control variables
. foreach x of varlist wealth_pca age place_attachment edu {
  2.         egen aux_`x' = median(`x')
  3.         gen `x'_median = 1 if `x' >= aux_`x'
  4.         replace `x'_median = 0 if `x' < aux_`x'
  5.         drop aux_`x'
  6. }

. *median splits of control variables
. foreach x of varlist income_average_hh age place_attach edu_yr {
  2.         egen aux_`x' = median(`x')
  3.         gen `x'_median = 1 if `x' >= aux_`x'
  4.         replace `x'_median = 0 if `x' < aux_`x'
  5.         drop aux_`x'
  6. }

. *Figure S4: Behavioral factors differ across mobility types
. *Risk taking
